text: During civil disturbance be like an adolescent camel who has neither a back strong enough for riding nor udders for milking.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who adopts greed as a habit devalues himself; he who discloses his hardship agrees to humiliation; and he who allows his tongue to overpower his soul debases the soul.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Miserliness is shame; cowardice is a defect; poverty disables an intelligent man from arguing his case; and a destitute person is a stranger in his home town.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Incapability is a catastrophe; endurance is bravery; abstinence is riches; self-restraint is a shield against sin; and the best companion is submission to Allah's will.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Knowledge is a venerable estate; good manners are new dresses; and thinking is a clear mirror.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The bosom of the wise is the safe of his secrets; cheerfulness is the bond of friendship; effective forbearance is the grave of shortcomings.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Charity is an effective cure, and the actions of people in their present life will be before their eyes in the next life.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: How wonderful is man that he sees with fat, talks with a piece of flesh, hears with a bone and breathes through a hole.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When this world advances towards anyone, it attributes to him the good of others; and when it turns away from him it deprives him of his own good.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Meet people in such a manner that if you die they should weep for you and if you live they should long for you.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you gain power over your adversary, pardon him by way of thanks for being able to overpower him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The most helpless of all men is he who cannot find a few brothers during his life, but still more helpless is he who finds such a brother but loses him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you get only small favours do not push them away through lack of gratefulness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who is abandoned by near ones is dear to remote ones.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Every mischief monger cannot even be reproved.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: All matters are subject to destiny, so much so that sometimes death results from effort.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Banish your old age, for when religion was confined to a few it needed distinction, but now that it has widened everyone is free in his action.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: They abandoned right but did not support wrong.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who gallops with loose rein collides with death.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Forgive the shortcomings of people of virtue and honour, because when they fall into error Allah raises them up.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The consequence of fear is disappointment and of bashfulness is frustration. Opportunity passes away like the cloud, so make use of good opportunities.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: We have a right. If it is allowed to us, well and good; otherwise we will ride on the hind part of the camel, even though the night journey may be long.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He whose deeds accord him a back position cannot be given a front position because of his lineage.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Rendering relief to the grief-stricken and providing comfort in hardship are among the means of atonement for great sins.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O son of Adam, when you see that your Lord bestows His favours on you while you are disobeying Him, you should fear Him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whenever a person conceals a thing in his heart it manifests itself through unintentional words from his tongue and the expressions of his face.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Keep walking in your sickness as long as you can.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The best abstemiousness is to conceal it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you are running away from the world and death is approaching, there is no question of delay in the encounter.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Fear! Fear! By Allah, He has hidden your sins so much that it is as though He has forgiven.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Faith stands on four supports: endurance, conviction, justice and striving. Whoever is eager for Paradise ignores the passions; whoever fears the Fire refrains from prohibited acts; whoever abstains from the world takes hardships lightly; and whoever anticipates death hastens towards good deeds.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The doer of good is better than the good itself, and the doer of evil is worse than the evil itself.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Be generous but not extravagant; be thrifty but not miserly.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The best of riches is the abandonment of desires.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If someone is quick in saying about people what they dislike, they speak about him that about which they have no knowledge.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever prolongs his desire ruins his actions.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: By Allah, this respect does not benefit your chiefs. You belabour yourselves in this world and earn misery for the next world by it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Learn four things from me: the richest of riches is intelligence; the biggest destitution is foolishness; the wildest wildness is vanity; and the best achievement is goodness of moral character.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Supererogatory worship cannot bring about nearness to Allah if it hampers the obligatory.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The tongue of the wise man is behind his heart, and the heart of the fool is behind his tongue.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The heart of a fool is in his mouth while the tongue of the wise man is in his heart.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: May Allah make your illness a means for writing off your sins, for there is no reward in sickness itself, but it erases sins and makes them fall like dried leaves.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He accepted Islam willingly, immigrated obediently, remained content with what sufficed him, was pleased with Allah, and lived the life of one who strives.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Blessed is the person who kept in mind the next life, acted so as to be able to render account, remained content with what sufficed him, and remained pleased with Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A believer will never hate me and a hypocrite will never love me.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The sin that displeases you is better in the view of Allah than the virtue which makes you proud.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The worth of a man is according to his courage, his truthfulness according to his balance of temper, his valour according to his self-respect, and his chasteness according to his sense of shame.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Victory is by determination; determination is by the turning over of thoughts; and thoughts are formed by guarding secrets.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Fear the attack of a noble person when he is hungry, and that of an ignoble person when he is satiated.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The hearts of people are like wild beasts. Whoever tames them, they would pounce upon him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: So long as your position is good, your defects will remain covered.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The most capable of pardoning is he who is the most powerful to punish.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Generosity is that which is by one's own initiative, because giving on being asked is either out of self-respect or to avoid rebuke.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is no wealth like wisdom, no destitution like ignorance, no inheritance like refinement, and no support like consultation.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Patience is of two kinds: patience over what pains you, and patience against what you covet.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: With wealth a strange land is a homeland, while with destitution even a homeland is a strange land.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Contentment is wealth that does not diminish.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Wealth is the fountainhead of passions.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever warns you is like one who gives you good tidings.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The tongue is a beast; if it is let loose, it devours.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Be cautious, for the sting that seems sweet can still wound.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If you are met with a greeting, give a better greeting in return. If a hand of help is extended to you, do a better favour in return, although the credit remains with the one who was first.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The interceder is the wing of the seeker.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The people of the world are like travellers who are being carried while they are asleep.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A lack of friends means strangeness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: To miss what one needs is easier than to beg from an inappropriate person.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not feel ashamed for giving little, because refusal is smaller than that.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Chastity is the adornment of destitution while gratefulness is the adornment of riches.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If what you aim at does not come about, then do not worry as to what you were.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You will not find an ignorant person but at one extreme or the other — one who neglects or one who exaggerates.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: As intelligence increases, speech decreases.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Time wears our bodies, renews desires, brings death nearer, and takes away aspirations. Whoever is successful with it encounters grief, and whoever misses its favours also undergoes hardships.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever places himself as a leader of the people should commence with educating his own self before educating others; and his teaching should be by his own conduct before teaching by the tongue.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The breath of a man is a step towards his death.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Every countable thing is to pass away and every expected thing must come about.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If matters get mixed up, then the last ones should be appreciated according to the first.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O world, get away from me. Your life is short, your importance is little, and your aspirations are base. Alas, the provision is little, the way is long, and the journey is far.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah ordered His people to act by free will and cautioned them; He placed easy obligations on them and not heavy ones; He gives much reward in return for little action.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Take wise points from wherever they may be, for a wise saying flutters in the bosom of the hypocrite until it comes out and settles with its like in the bosom of the believer.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A wise saying is a lost article of the believer; therefore, get wise sayings even from people of hypocrisy.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The worth of every man is in his attainments.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: No one should repose hope save in his Lord; no one should fear anything save his sin; no one should feel ashamed of saying 'I do not know' when he does not know; and no one should feel ashamed of learning what he does not know.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I am below what you express and above what you feel in your heart.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The survivors of the sword are large in number and have a large progeny.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever abandons saying 'I do not know' meets his destruction.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I love the opinion of an old man more than the determination of a young man.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I wonder about the man who loses hope despite the possibility of seeking forgiveness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Two sources of deliverance from punishment existed: one has been raised up, the Messenger of Allah; the other remains, the seeking of forgiveness. So adhere to it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If a man sets right matters between himself and Allah, then Allah sets right matters between him and other people; and whoever is a preacher for himself is protected by Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The perfect jurist is he who does not let people lose hope from the mercy of Allah, does not make them despondent of His kindness, and does not make them feel safe from His punishment.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: These hearts get weary as bodies get weary; so look for beautiful wise sayings for them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The lowest form of knowledge is that which remains on the tongue, and the highest is that which manifests itself through the action of the limbs and organs.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: None should seek protection from trial, for no one is free of it; rather, seek protection from misguiding trials, for wealth and children are a test.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Good is not that your wealth and progeny should be much; good is that your knowledge should be much, your forbearance great, and that you vie with people in worship.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Action accompanied by fear of Allah does not fail — and how can a thing fail that has been accepted.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The friend of the Prophet is he who obeys Allah, even though he has no blood relationship; and the enemy of the Prophet is he who disobeys Allah, even though he has near kinship.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Sleeping in a state of firm belief is better than praying in a state of doubtfulness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you hear a tradition, test it according to the criterion of intelligence, not that of mere hearing, for relaters of knowledge are numerous but those who guard it are few.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Our saying 'we are Allah's' is an admission of His Majesty over us, and our saying 'to Him we shall return' is an admission of our being mortal.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O my God, You know me better than myself, and I know myself better than they do. Make us better than what they think, and forgive us what they do not know.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Fulfilment of others' needs becomes a lasting virtue in three ways: regarding it small so it grows big, concealing it so it manifests itself, and doing it quickly so it becomes pleasant.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A time will come when high positions are given only to those who defame others, when the vicious are regarded as witty and the just are regarded as weak.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This world and the next are two enemies against each other and two paths in different directions. Whoever loves this world hates the next and is its enemy.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Blessed are those who abstain from this world and are eager for the next; they regard this earth as a floor, its dust as their bed, and its water as their perfume.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah has laid down obligations you should not ignore, limits you should not transgress, and prohibitions you should not violate; and He kept silent on some things, not through forgetfulness, so do not burden yourself with them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If people give up something relating to their religion to set right their worldly affairs, Allah will inflict upon them something more harmful than that.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Often the ignorance of a learned man ruins him, while the knowledge he has does not avail him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: In man there is a piece of flesh — the heart — and it is the strangest thing in him. It has a store of wisdom and things contrary to wisdom; every shortness harms it and every excess injures it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: We are like the pillow in the middle: he who lags behind must come forward to meet it, and he who exceeds the bounds must return to it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: No one can establish the rule of Allah except he who shows no relenting in the matter of right, who does not behave like wrongdoers, and who does not run after objects of greed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Even if a mountain had loved me, it would have crumbled down.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever loves us, the Household of the Prophet, should be prepared to face destitution.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: No wealth is more profitable than wisdom; no loneliness is more estranging than vanity; no honour is like fear of Allah; no companion is like goodness of moral character; and no support is more reliable than consultation.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When virtue is in vogue, suspecting a person from whom nothing evil has been seen is injustice; when vice is in vogue, having a good idea of another person is to fling oneself into peril.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: How can he be whom life is driving towards death, whose state of health can change into sickness any moment, and who is to be caught by death from his place of safety?
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Many a person is given time through good treatment, many are deceived because their sins are veiled, and many are enamoured by good talk about themselves; Allah tries no one as seriously as the one He allows time.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Two categories of persons will face ruin on account of me: he who loves me with exaggeration, and he who hates me intensely.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: To miss an opportunity brings about grief.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The example of the world is like a serpent: soft to the touch, but its inside is full of venom. The ignorant is attracted to it, but the wise keeps on guard against it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: We spend whatever we get and are very generous in offering ourselves; they are more numerous and more contriving, while we are more eloquent and well-wishing.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: What a difference between two kinds of actions: an act whose pleasure passes away but its ill consequence remains, and an act whose hardship passes away but its reward stays.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Is it that death has been ordained only for others? Those whom we see departing will not come back; we lay them in their graves and enjoy their estate as if we will live for good after them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Blessed is he who humbles himself, whose livelihood is pure, whose heart is chaste, whose habits are virtuous, who spends his savings in charity, and who prevents his tongue from speaking nonsense.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The jealousy of a woman is misplaced, while the protective jealousy of a man is a part of belief.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Islam is submission; submission is conviction; conviction is affirmation; affirmation is acknowledgement; acknowledgement is discharge of obligations; and discharge of obligations is action.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I wonder at the miser who speeds towards the very destitution he wants to escape and misses the very ease he covets; he lives like the poor but will be held to account like the rich.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever falls short of actions falls into grief, and Allah has nothing to do with one who spares nothing of his wealth and self in His name.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Guard against cold in its beginning and welcome it towards its end, for it affects bodies as it affects plants — in the beginning it destroys them, but in the end it gives them fresh leaves.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Greatness of the Creator appreciated by you would belittle the creatures in your view.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O people of the lonely houses and gloomy graves, you have gone ahead and we are following you. The houses you left are inhabited, the properties distributed — what is the news where you are?
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This world is a house of truth for him who appreciates it, a place of safety for him who understands it, and a house of instruction for him who draws instruction from it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is an angel of Allah who calls out every day: Beget children for death, collect wealth for destruction, and raise construction for ruin.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This world is a place for transit, not a place for stay. People herein are of two kinds: one who sold his self and ruined it, and one who purchased his self and freed it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A friend is not a friend unless he affords protection to his comrade on three occasions: in adversity, in absence, and at death.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who is allowed to pray is not deprived of its response; he who is allowed to repent is not deprived of its acceptance; he who is allowed to seek forgiveness is not deprived of forgiveness; and he who is allowed to be grateful is not deprived of an increase in favours.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Prayer is a means of seeking nearness to Allah for the God-fearing; for everything there is a levy, and the levy of the body is fasting.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Seek livelihood by giving alms.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who is sure of a good return is generous in giving.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Assistance is allowed according to need.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who is moderate does not become destitute.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A small family is one of the two ways of securing ease.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Loving one another is half of wisdom.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Grief is half of old age.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Endurance comes according to the affliction. He who beats his hand on his thigh in his affliction ruins all his good actions.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Many a fasting person gains nothing from his fast but hunger and thirst, and many an offerer of prayers gains nothing but wakefulness and hardship. The sleep and the eating of the wise is far better.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Protect your belief by charity, guard your wealth by paying Allah's share, and ward off the waves of calamity by praying.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Knowledge is better than wealth: knowledge guards you while you have to guard wealth; wealth decreases by spending, while knowledge multiplies by spending.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Man is hidden under his tongue.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who does not know his own worth is ruined.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not be like one who hopes for the next life without action and delays repentance by lengthening desires; who speaks like the ascetics but acts like those eager for the world; who preaches at length but does not accept any preaching for himself.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Every human being has to meet the end, sweet or bitter.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Every comer has to return, and after returning it is as though he never existed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The endurer does not miss success, although it may take a long time.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who agrees with the action of a group is as though he joins them in it; and every one who joins in wrong commits two sins — one for the act, and one for agreeing with it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Adhere to contracts and entrust their fulfilment to steadfast persons.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: On you lies the obligation of obedience to the person about whom you cannot plead the excuse of ignorance.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Surely you have been made to see, if only you care to see; you have been guided, if only you care to take guidance; and you have been made to hear, if only you care to lend your ears.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Admonish your brother by good behaviour towards him, and ward off his evil by favouring him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who puts himself in conditions of ill repute should not blame those who entertain bad ideas about him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever obtains authority usually adopts partiality.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who acts solely according to his own opinion gets ruined, and he who consults other people shares in their understanding.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who guards his secrets retains control in his own hands.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Destitution is the greatest death.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who fulfils the right of a man who does not fulfil his right is as though he worships him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There should be no obeying anyone against the commands of Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: No person is to be blamed for delay in securing his own right; blame lies on him who takes what he is not entitled to.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Vanity prevents progress.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The Day of Judgement is near and our mutual company is short.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: For the man who has eyes, the dawn has already appeared.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Abstention from sin is easier than seeking forgiveness afterwards.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Many a single eating prevents several eatings.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: People are enemies of what they do not know.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who considers many opinions understands the pitfalls.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who sharpens the teeth of anger for the sake of Allah acquires the strength to kill the stalwarts of wrong.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you are afraid of something, dive straight into it, because the intensity of abstaining from it is worse than what you are afraid of.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The means to secure high authority is breadth of chest — generosity.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Rebuke the evildoer by rewarding the good-doer.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Cut away evil from the chest of others by snatching it away from your own chest.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Stubbornness destroys good advice.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Greed is a lasting slavery.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The result of neglect is shame, while the result of far-sightedness is safety.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is no good in keeping quiet about an issue of wisdom, just as there is no good in speaking out an unintelligent thing.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If there are two different calls, then one of them must be towards misguidance.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I have never entertained doubt about right since I was shown it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I have neither spoken a lie nor have I been told a lie; I have neither deviated nor have I been made to deviate.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who takes the lead in oppression will have to bite his hand in repentance tomorrow.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The departure from this world is imminent.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever turns away from right is ruined.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If patience does not give relief to a man, impatience kills him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: How strange! Could the caliphate be through the Prophet's companionship but not through both his companionship and his kinship?
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: In this world man is the target towards which the arrows of death fly; with every drink there is suffocation and with every morsel there is choking; no one gets anything unless he loses something else.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O son of Adam, whatever you earn beyond your basic needs you are only a treasurer of it for others.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Hearts have a tendency to advance and retreat. Approach them for action when they are eager and advancing, for if hearts are forced they will be blinded.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When shall I vent my anger — when I am unable to take revenge and it is said to me 'better you endure,' or when I have power to take revenge and it is said to me 'better forgive'?
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This is what the misers used to be niggardly about.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The wealth that teaches you a lesson does not go to waste.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Hearts become tired as bodies become tired; you should therefore search for beautiful sayings for them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It is a word of truth by which wrong is intended.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: These are the people who, when they assemble, are overwhelming, but when they disperse cannot be recognized.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Woe to the faces who are seen only on foul occasions.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: With every individual there are two angels who protect him; when destiny approaches they let it have its way. The appointed time is a protective shield.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You will have a share in strengthening the cause and in affording assistance, and you will both be helping at the time of need and hardship.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O people, fear Allah, Who hears you when you speak and knows when you conceal. Prepare for death, which will overtake you even if you run, catch you even if you stay, and remember you even if you forget it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If someone is not grateful to you, that should not prevent you from good actions, for one will feel grateful who has not even drawn benefit from it, and his gratefulness will exceed the ingratitude of the denier.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Every container gets narrower according to what is placed in it — except the container of knowledge, which expands instead.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The first reward the exerciser of forbearance gets is that people become his helpers against the ignorant.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If you cannot forbear, feign to do so, for it is seldom that a man likens himself to a group and does not become one of them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever takes account of his self is benefited, and whoever remains neglectful of it suffers; whoever fears remains safe; whoever takes instruction perceives; and whoever perceives gains understanding.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The world will bend towards us after having been refractory, as the biting she-camel bends towards its young.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Fear Allah like one who extracts himself from worldly affairs, makes ready, then acts quickly, keeping his eye on the end of his journey and the place of his return.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Generosity is the protector of honour; forbearance is the bridle of the fool; forgiveness is the levy of success; endurance braves calamities; and the best contentment is to give up desires.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A man's vanity for himself is one of the enemies of his intelligence.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Ignore pain and grief; you will ever be happy.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The tree whose trunk is soft has thick branches.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Opposition destroys good counsel.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who gives generously achieves position.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Through change of circumstances the mettle of men is known.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Jealousy by a friend means a defect in his love.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Most of the deficiency of intelligence occurs under the flash of greed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is no justice in passing a verdict by relying on probability.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The worst provision for the Day of Judgement is high-handedness over people.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The highest act of a noble person is to ignore what he knows.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whomever modesty clothes with its dress, people cannot see his defects.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Excess of silence produces awe; justice results in more friends; generosity heightens position; with humility blessings abound; and by facing hardships leadership is achieved.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It is strange that the jealous do not feel jealous about bodily health.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The greedy is in the shackles of disgrace.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Belief means appreciation with the heart, acknowledgement with the tongue, and action with the limbs.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who is sorrowful for this world is displeased with the dispensation of Allah; he who complains of a calamity complains of his Lord.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Contentment is as good as an estate, and goodness of moral character is as good as a blessing. A good and pure life is contentment.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Be a sharer with him who has an abundant livelihood, for he is more likely to get more riches and to secure an increase of the share therein.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Justice means equitable distribution, and benevolence means favour.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who gives with his short hand is given by a long hand.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not call out for fighting, but if you are called to it, do respond; for the caller to fighting is a rebel, and the rebel deserves destruction.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Since the woman is protective, she will not allow access to herself; since she is careful, she will preserve her own property and the property of her husband.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The wise is one who places things in their proper positions. As for the ignorant — I have already described him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: By Allah, this world of yours is more lowly in my view than the leftover bone of a pig in the hand of a leper.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A group worshipped Allah out of desire for reward — that is the worship of traders; another out of fear — that is the worship of slaves; and another out of gratefulness — that is the worship of free men.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Excess of attachment to the world ends only in regret; one cannot do without it, yet one must not be enslaved by it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who obeys laziness loses his rights, and he who believes the backbiter loses his friend.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: One ill-gotten piece of stone in a house is a guarantee for its ruin.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The day of the oppressed over the oppressor will be severer than the day of the oppressor over the oppressed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Fear Allah to some degree though it be little; and set a curtain between you and Allah though it be thin.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When replies are numerous, the correct point remains obscure.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: In every blessing there is a right of Allah. If one discharges that right, Allah increases the blessing; if one falls short, one stands in danger of losing it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When capability increases, desire decreases.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Keep on guard against the slipping away of blessings, for not everything that runs away comes back.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Generosity prompts good more than regard for kinship.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If a person has a good idea about you, make his idea come true.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The best act is that which you have to force yourself to do.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I came to know Allah through the breaking of determinations and the change of intentions.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The bitterness of this world is the sweetness of the next, while the sweetness of this world is the bitterness of the next.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah laid down belief for purification from polytheism, prayer for purification from vanity, charity as a means of livelihood, fasting as a trial of sincerity, and good leadership for the orderliness of the community.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Truth defended dishonestly does not last; the one who relies on falsehood is overtaken quickly.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O son of Adam, be your own executor in the matter of your property, and do with it now whatever you would want done with it after your death.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Anger is a kind of madness, because the victim repents afterwards; and if he does not repent, his madness is confirmed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Health of body comes from paucity of envy.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Direct your people to go out by day to achieve noble traits and by night to meet the needs of those who might be sleeping; for whoever pleases another's heart, Allah creates from that joy a kindness that drives away his hardship.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you fall into destitution, trade with Allah through charity.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Faithfulness with faithless people is faithlessness with Allah, while faithlessness with faithless people is faithfulness with Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is many a man being gradually brought towards punishment by good treatment, and many a man in illusion because of good talk about him; there is no greater ordeal than the giving of time.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You cannot be enough for me against yourselves, so how can you be enough for me against others? It is as though I am led and they are the leaders, or I am the subject and they are the rulers.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You have looked below yourself but not above yourself, and so you have been confused; you have not known right, so you cannot recognize the righteous.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The holder of authority is like the rider on a lion — he is envied for his position, but he well knows his danger.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do good to the bereaved ones of others, so that good is done to your bereaved ones also.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When the utterance of the wise is to the point it serves as a cure, but if it is wrong it proves like an illness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: An utterance is like a fluttering prey — it may be grappled with by someone but missed by others.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O son of Adam, do not inflict the worry of the day that has not yet come on the day that has already come; for if that day be in your life, Allah will bring its livelihood with it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Have love for your friend up to a limit, for it is possible he may turn into your enemy some day; and hate your enemy up to a limit, for it is possible he may turn into your friend some day.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There are two kinds of workers: one who works in this world for this world, and one who works in this world for what is to come; the latter gets both the benefits together.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah left the ornaments where they were — not by omission, nor were they unknown to Him. Therefore, leave them where Allah and His Prophet placed them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: As for the one who is the property of public money, there is no punishment, for it means one property of Allah having taken another; as for the other, the penalty stands.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If my steps acquire firmness out of these slippery places, I will alter several things.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Know with full conviction that Allah has not fixed for any person more livelihood than what has been ordained, even though his means be great and his craving intense.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not turn your knowledge into ignorance or your conviction into doubt. When you gain knowledge, act; and when you acquire conviction, proceed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Greed takes a person to the watering place but gets him back without letting him drink; it undertakes responsibility but does not fulfil it. Desires blind the eyes of understanding.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O my God, I seek Your protection from appearing good in the eyes of people while my inward self is sinful before You.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I swear by Him Who let us pass the dark night, after which there was a bright day, that it did not happen.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A small action which is continued with regularity is more beneficial than a long one performed with disgust.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When optional issues stand in the way of obligatory ones, abandon them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever keeps in view the distance of the journey remains prepared.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Perception by the eyes is not real observation, because the eyes sometimes deceive; but wisdom does not deceive whomsoever it counsels.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Between you and the preaching there is a curtain of deception.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The ignorant among you are excessive in their deeds, while your learned fall short.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Knowledge dispels the excuse of those who advance excuses.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He whom death overtakes early seeks more time, while he whose death is deferred puts forth excuses for postponement.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: For every thing to which people say 'how good,' there is an evil hidden in this world.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Destiny is a dark path — do not tread upon it; a deep ocean — do not dive in it; and the secret of Allah — do not take trouble about knowing it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When Allah intends to humiliate a person, He denies him knowledge.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I had a brother in faith; the world was humble in his eyes; he did not long for what he did not get; if he got a thing he would not ask for more; most of his time he was silent; and when he spoke, he silenced the other speakers.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Even if Allah had not warned of chastisement for disobedience, it would be obligatory, by way of gratefulness for His favours, that He should not be disobeyed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If you grieve over your son, the blood relationship deserves it; but if you endure, Allah provides recompense for every affliction. If you endure, matters move as ordained and you are rewarded; if you lose patience, matters still move, but you bear the burden.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Endurance is good except about you; fretting is bad except over you; and the affliction about you is great, while every other affliction before or after it is small.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not associate with a fool, for he will beautify his actions before you and long that you too be like him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The distance between East and West is one day's travelling for the sun.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Your friends are three and your enemies are three: your friend, your friend's friend, and your enemy's enemy; your enemy, your friend's enemy, and your enemy's friend.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You are like one who pierces a spear through himself in order to kill the person sitting behind him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: How many are the objects of lessons, but how few the taking of lessons.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who goes too far in quarrelling is a sinner; if one falls short in it, one is oppressed; and it is difficult for a quarreller to fear Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I am not worried by a sin after which I get time to offer prayer in two units and beg safety from Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah will conduct the accounting of all persons, despite their large number, just as He provides them livelihood despite their large number.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Your messenger is the interpreter of your intelligence, while your letter is the most eloquent thing that speaks for you.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The person afflicted with hardship is not in greater need of praying than the one who has been spared affliction but is not immune from it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: People are the progeny of the world, and no one can be blamed for loving his mother.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The destitute is the messenger of Allah: whoever denies him denies Allah, and whoever gives him gives Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A self-respecting man never commits adultery.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The fixed limit of life is enough to remain watchful.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A man can sleep on the death of his child, but cannot sleep at the loss of property.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Mutual affection between fathers creates a relationship between the sons; and relationship is more in need of affection than affection is of relationship.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Be afraid of the ideas of believers, for Allah has put truth on their tongues.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The belief of a person cannot be regarded as true unless his trust in what is with Allah is more than his trust in what he himself has.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If you are speaking a lie, may Allah afflict you with white spots which even the turban may not cover.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Sometimes hearts move forward and sometimes backward. When they move forward, get them to perform the optional acts; when they move backward, keep them confined to obligatories only.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The Qur'an contains news about the past, foretellings about the future, and commandments for the present.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Throw a stone back from where it comes to you, for evil can be met only with evil.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Put cotton in the inkpot, keep the nib of your pen long, leave space between the lines, and close up the letters — this is good for the beauty of the writing.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I am the leader of the believers, while wealth is the leader of the wicked.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: We did not differ about him but after him; whereas your feet had not yet dried from the river when you asked your prophet to make for you a god.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: I never confronted a person but he helped me against himself.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O my son, I fear destitution for you, so seek Allah's protection from it; for destitution is deficiency of religion, perplexity of intelligence, and a cause of hatred.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Ask me in order to gain understanding, but do not ask me seeking to create confusion; for the ignorant who tries to learn is like the learned, but the learned who tries to confuse is like the ignorant.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You may advise me, but then I have to decide; and if I act against your advice, you must still follow me.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do your women have control over you in the weeping that I hear? Do you not stop them from this crying? Walk back — the walking of a man like you with one like me is mischief for the ruler and disgrace for the believer.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Satan the deceiver, and the inner spirit that leads to evil, deceived them through false hopes, made sins easy for them, promised them victory, and threw them into the Fire.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Beware of disobeying Allah in solitude, for the witness of that situation is also the judge.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Our grief over him is as great as their joy for it — except that they have lost an enemy and we have lost a friend.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The age up to which Allah accepts any excuse for a human being is sixty years.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He whom sin overpowers is not victorious, and he who secures victory by evil is in fact vanquished.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah has fixed the livelihood of the destitute in the wealth of the rich; so whenever a destitute remains hungry, it is because some rich person has denied him his share.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Not to be in need of putting forth an excuse is better than putting forth a true excuse.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The least right of Allah on you is that you should not make use of His favours in committing His sins.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When the disabled fall short in obeying Allah, it is a good opportunity given by Allah for the intelligent to perform such acts.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The sovereign is the watchman of Allah on earth.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A believer has a cheerful face and a sorrowful heart; a very broad chest and a very humble self; he hates high position and dislikes renown; his silence is much and his time is occupied; he is grateful and enduring.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If a man could see the end of his life and his final fate, he would begin hating desires and their deception.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There are two sharers in the property of every person: the successors and accidents.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The person who is approached with a request is free until he promises.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who prays but does not exert effort is like one who shoots without a bowstring.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Knowledge is of two kinds: that which is absorbed and that which is just heard. That which is heard does not give benefit unless it is absorbed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Correctness of decision goes together with power; it emerges with the other's emergence and disappears when the other disappears.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The beauty of destitution is chastity, and the beauty of riches is gratefulness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The day of justice over the oppressor will be severer than the day of oppression over the oppressed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The biggest wealth is that one should not set one's eye on what others possess.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Utterances are preserved, the inner selves are tried, and every soul is held in pledge for what it earned. People are deficient and meddled with, save those whom Allah protects.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O people, fear Allah; for many a man hopes for what he does not get, builds a house he does not live in, and collects what he soon leaves behind.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Lack of access to sins is also a kind of chastity.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The dignity of your face is solid, but begging dissolves it away; therefore, look carefully before whom you dissolve it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: To praise more than what is due is sycophancy; to do it less is either inability to speak or envy.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The most serious sin is that which the doer considers light.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who sees his own shortcomings abstains from looking into others' shortcomings; he who feels happy with the livelihood Allah provides does not grieve over what he misses.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The oppressor has three signs: he oppresses his superior by disobeying him, his junior by imposing his authority, and he supports other oppressors.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: At the extremity of hardship comes relief, and at the tightening of the chains of tribulation comes ease.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not devote much of your activity to your wife and children; for if they are lovers of Allah, He will not leave His lovers uncared for; and if they are otherwise, why keep yourself busy about them?
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The greatest defect is to regard as a defect in others that which is present in yourself.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not say 'congratulations for a rider of horses'; rather say: you have occasion to be grateful to the Giver, and may you be blessed in what you have been given.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This building speaks of your riches — silver coins showing forth their faces.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If a man is left in his house and the door is closed, his livelihood will reach him from whatever way his death reaches him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This thing has not started with you, nor does it end with you. Your fellow was used to journeying; think of him still journeying — either he will rejoin you, or you will rejoin him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O people, let Allah see you fearing at the time of happiness, just as you fear Him at the time of distress; for ease can be a slow approach to trial.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O slaves of desire, cut it short, for whoever leans on the world gets nothing from it but the pain of hardships. O people, take charge of training your own souls.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not regard an expression uttered by anyone as evil if you can find it capable of bearing some good.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If you have a need from Allah, begin by seeking His blessing on His Messenger, then ask your need; for Allah is too generous to grant one request and deny the other.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He who is jealous of his honour should keep away from quarrelling.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: To make haste before the proper time, or to delay after the opportunity, is in either case folly.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not ask about things which may not happen, because you have enough to worry about with what does happen.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Imagination is a clear mirror, and the taking of lessons is a warning counsellor; it is enough discipline for yourself to avoid what you consider bad in others.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Knowledge is associated with action; he who knows should act, for knowledge calls for action — if there is a response, well and good; otherwise it departs from him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O people, the wealth of this world is broken orts that bring an epidemic; therefore keep off its grazing land. Its part enough for subsistence is more blissful than its riches.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah has laid down reward for obedience and punishment for sin in order to save people from His chastisement and to drive them towards Paradise.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A time will come when nothing remains of the Qur'an except its writing, and nothing of Islam except its name; the mosques will be busy with construction but desolate of guidance.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O people, fear Allah; man has not been created for naught so that he may waste himself, nor has he been left uncared for so that he may commit nonsensical acts.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is no distinction higher than Islam, no honour more honourable than fear of Allah, no asylum better than self-restraint, no intercessor more effective than repentance, and no treasure more precious than contentment.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The mainstay of religion and the world are four: the scholar who acts on his knowledge, the ignorant who is not ashamed to learn, the generous who is not niggardly, and the destitute who does not sell his next life for worldly benefit.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever observes wrong with his heart and disapproves it is safe; whoever disapproves it with his tongue is rewarded; and whoever disapproves it with his sword, so the word of Allah may remain superior, has caught hold of the path of guidance.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Among them is he who disapproves evil with hand, tongue, and heart — he has perfected the virtuous habits; and among them is he who disapproves with none of these — he is a dead man among the living.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The first fighting with which you will be overpowered is fighting with your hands, then with your tongues, then with your hearts.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Certainly, right is weighty and wholesome, while wrong is light and unwholesome.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not feel safe from the punishment of Allah even about the best of this community, nor lose hope of His mercy even for its worst.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Miserliness contains all other evil vices, and is the rein with which one can be led to every evil.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O son of Adam, livelihood is of two kinds: that which you seek, and that which seeks you; if you do not reach it, it will come to you. So do not turn your one day's worry into a year's worry.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Many a man faces a day after which he finds no day; and many a man is envied in the early part of the night but is wept over by bewailing women in its later part.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Words are in your control until you have not uttered them; but when you have spoken them, you are under their control. Therefore guard your tongue as you guard your gold and silver.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not say what you do not know; rather, do not say all that you know; for Allah has laid obligations on all your limbs by which He will argue against you on the Day of Judgement.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When you are strong, be strong in obeying Allah; and when you are weak, be weak in committing sins against Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Leaning towards this world, despite what you see of it, is folly; lagging behind in good deeds, when you are convinced of their reward, is obvious loss; and trusting everyone before trying them is weakness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It is a proof of the baseness of the world before Allah that He is disobeyed only herein, and His favours cannot be achieved except by abandoning it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: One who is in search of something will obtain it, or at least a part of it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: That good is no good after which there is the Fire, and that hardship is no hardship after which there is Paradise; every bliss other than Paradise is inferior, and every calamity other than the Fire is comfort.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Worse than destitution is the ailment of the body, and worse than bodily ailment is the disease of the heart; better than plenty of wealth is the health of the body, and better than that is the chastity of the heart.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whomever his action detains behind, his lineage cannot put him forward.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The believer's time has three periods: communion with Allah, managing his livelihood, and free enjoyment of what is lawful and pleasant.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Abstain from the world, so that Allah may show you its real evils; and do not be neglectful, for you will not be neglected.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Speak, so that you may be known; for man is hidden under his tongue.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Take of the world's favours whatever comes to you, and turn away from what turns away from you; and if you cannot do so, then be moderate in your seeking.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Many an expression is more effective than an attack.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Every small thing that one is contented with is sufficient.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Let it be death, but not humiliation; let it be little, but not through others. The world has two days: one for you and one against you. When it is for you, do not be proud; and when it is against you, endure.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The best scent is musk: its weight is light, while its odour is fragrant.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Put off boasting, give up self-conceit, and remember your grave.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The right of the father on the child is that he obey him in everything except disobedience to Allah; and the right of the child on the father is that he give him a good name, train him well, and teach him the Qur'an.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The effect of sight is real, charm is real, and auguring good is real; but auguring evil is not real. Scent gives pleasure, honey gives pleasure, and looking at greenery gives pleasure.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Nearness with people in their manners brings about safety from their evil.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: You have started flying soon after growing your first feathers, and grumbling before reaching maturity.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever hankers after contradictory aims gets no means of success.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: We are not master of anything along with Allah, and we are not master of anything save what He makes us master of; when He gives us charge of something, He assigns us duties, and when He takes it away, He removes those duties.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Leave him alone, for he has taken from religion only what brought the world close to him, and he wilfully clouded matters to make doubts a cover for his shortcomings.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: How good it is for the rich to show humility before the poor, seeking the reward of Allah; but better than that is the dignity of the poor before the rich, with trust in Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah does not grant wisdom to a person except that some day He will save him from ruin by means of it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever clashes with the truth will be knocked down by it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The heart is the book of the eye.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Fear of Allah is the chief trait of human character.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do not use the sharpness of your tongue against Him Who gave you the power to speak, nor the eloquence of your speech against Him Who set you on the right path.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It is enough discipline for yourself that you abstain from what you dislike in others.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: One should endure like free people; otherwise one should forget like the ignorant.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Either endure like noble people, or else you will forget like animals.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The world deceives, harms, and passes away; Allah did not approve it as a reward for His lovers nor as a punishment for His enemies. Its people are like riders who, as soon as they alight, the driver calls and they march off.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O my son, do not leave anything of this world behind you, for you leave it for one of two: one who uses it in obeying Allah and becomes happy by what was evil for you, or one who uses it in disobeying Allah, and you become his helper in sin.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Asking forgiveness is the rank of the high ones: it stands on six supports — regret for the past, firm resolve never to return to it, discharging the rights of people, fulfilling neglected obligations, and making the body taste the pain of obedience.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Clemency is like a kinsfolk.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: How wretched the son of Adam is: his death is hidden, his ailments concealed, his actions preserved; the bite of a mosquito pains him, choking causes his death, and sweat gives him a bad smell.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When anyone of you sees a woman who attracts him, he should turn to his own wife; for she is a woman like his wife.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It is enough wisdom for you that it makes clear to you the path of your going astray from the path of your guidance.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Do good and do not regard any part of it small, for its small is big and its little is much; and let none of you say another is more deserving of doing good than I.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever sets right his inward self, Allah sets right his outward self; whoever works for his religion, Allah accomplishes for him the affairs of his world.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Forbearance is a curtain that covers, and wisdom is a sharp sword; so conceal the weaknesses of your conduct with forbearance, and kill your desires with your wisdom.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There are creatures of Allah whom He particularizes with favours for the benefit of the people; He keeps the favours in their hands so long as they give them to others, but when they deny them, He takes the favours away.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It does not behove a man to trust in two things: health and riches; for you may see him healthy, and then he falls sick; and rich, and then he turns destitute.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever complains of a need to a believer, it is as though he complained to Allah; but whoever complains of it to an unbeliever, it is as though he complained about Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: It is a feast for him whose fasting Allah accepts and whose prayers He appreciates; and every day in which no sin against Allah is committed is a feast.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The greatest regret on the Day of Judgement will be felt by the man who earned wealth through sinful ways, which is then inherited by one who spends it in obeying Allah and is awarded Paradise for it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The worst in transaction and the most unsuccessful in striving is the man who wears out his body in seeking his hopes, but fate does not help him, so he leaves the world in sorrow and faces its ill consequences in the next.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Livelihood is of two kinds: the seeker and the sought. He who hankers after this world, death traces him until it turns him out of it; but he who hankers after the next world, worldly ease itself seeks him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The lovers of Allah look at the inward side of the world while others look at its outward side; they busy themselves with its remoter benefits while others busy themselves with the immediate.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Remember that pleasures pass away while the consequences stay.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Try a man, and you will come to dislike him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah does not open the door of gratitude for a person and then close the door of increase upon him; nor open the door of prayer and close the door of acceptance; nor open the door of repentance and close the door of forgiveness.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The most appropriate person for an honourable position is he who descends from people of honour.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Justice puts things in their places, while generosity takes them out of their directions; justice is a general caretaker, while generosity is a particular benefit; therefore justice is the superior of the two.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: People are enemies of what they do not know.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The whole of asceticism is confined between two expressions of the Qur'an: do not grieve over what escapes you, and do not be overjoyed by what He grants you.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: What a breaker is sleep for the resolutions of the day.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Governing power is the proving ground of people.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: No town has greater right on you than another; the best town for you is the one that bears you.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Malik — and what a man Malik was. By Allah, if he had been a mountain he would have been a great one, and if he had been a stone he would have been hard; no horseman could reach it and no bird could fly over it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A little that lasts is better than much that brings grief.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If a man possesses one revealing quality, wait and see his other qualities.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Losing your camels to the discharge of obligations is the most praiseworthy way of losing them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever trades without knowing the rules of religious law will be involved in usury.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever regards small distresses as big, Allah involves him in big ones.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whoever keeps his own respect in view finds his desires light to give up.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Whenever a man cracks a joke, he separates away a bit from his wit.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Your turning away from one who inclines towards you is a loss of your share of advantage; and your inclining towards one who turns away from you is a humiliation for yourself.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Riches and destitution will be realized only after presentation before Allah.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: He remained a man from our house until his ill-fated son grew up.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: What has the son of Adam to do with vanity? His origin is a drop of semen and his end is a carcass; he cannot feed himself, nor ward off death.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The poets did not run in one single field such that the height of their glory could be known; but if it must be done, then it is the misled king.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Is there no free man who can leave this chewed morsel of the world to those who like it? There is no price for your selves except Paradise; so do not sell yourselves except for it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Two greedy persons are never satiated: the seeker of knowledge and the seeker of this world.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Belief means you should prefer truth when it harms you over falsehood when it benefits you; that your words should not exceed your knowledge; and that you fear Allah when speaking about others.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Destiny holds sway over our planning, until effort itself brings about ruin.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Forbearance and endurance are twins, and they are the product of high courage.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Backbiting is the tool of the helpless.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Many a man gets into mischief because of being spoken well of.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: This world has been created for something other than itself, and has not been created for its own sake.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: When a people fall into differences among themselves, even the weak can overpower them.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: By Allah, they nurtured Islam with their generous hands and eloquent tongues, as a year-old calf is nurtured.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The eye is the strap of the rear — when it sleeps, the bag cannot retain what is in it.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A ruler took charge of them; he remained upright and made them upright, until the entire religion put its bosom on the ground.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: A severe time will come upon people, wherein the rich will seize possessions with their teeth, though not commanded to do so; the wicked will rise and the virtuous will be brought low.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Two types of persons will fall into ruin over me: the one who loves with exaggeration, and the one who lays false and baseless blames.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Unity means that you do not subject Him to the limitations of your imagination; and justice means that you do not lay any blame on Him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: There is no good in silence over matters of wisdom, just as there is no good in speaking with ignorance.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: O my God, send us rain by submissive clouds, not by unruly ones.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Dye is a way of adornment, while we are in a state of grief.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The one who strives and is martyred in the way of Allah does not earn a greater reward than he who remains chaste despite having the means; the chaste one is near to being one of the angels.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Contentment is a wealth that does not exhaust.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Act on justice and keep away from violence and injustice; for violence drives people to forsake their homes, while injustice prompts them to take up arms.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The worst sin is that which the one who commits it takes lightly.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: Allah did not make it obligatory on the ignorant to learn until He had made it obligatory on the learned to teach.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: The worst comrade is the one for whom formality has to be observed.
author: Imam Ali (AS)

text: If a believer enrages his brother, it means that he has parted from him.
author: Imam Ali (AS)
